contentBlocks.original:
॥ चमकप्रश्नः ॥ - 1st Anuvaka
ॐ अग्ना॑विष्णो स॒जोष॑से॒माव॑र्धन्तु वा॒-ङ्गिरः॑ ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
camakapraśnaḥ - 1st Anuvaka
ōṃ agnā̍viṣṇō sa̠jōṣa̍sē̠māva̍rdhantu vā̠-ṅgira̍ḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: May the hymns of praise offered together in harmony for Agni and Vishnu increase and grow.
contentBlocks.meanings: oṃ agnāviṣṇū sajoṣasemā vardhantu vāṃ giraḥ: May the hymns of praise for Agni and Vishnu, offered together in harmony, increase and grow.

contentBlocks.translation: May you (Agni and Vishnu) arrive with radiant splendor and blessings of wealth and strength.
contentBlocks.meanings:
dyumnairvājebhirāgatam: May you (Agni and Vishnu) arrive with radiant splendor and blessings of wealth and strength.
dyumnair: With brilliance or splendor.
vājebhiḥ: With wealth or blessings (often referring to strength, energy, or sustenance).
āgatam: Arrive or come.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have both strength and progeny.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vājaśca me prasavaśca me: May I have both strength and progeny.
vājaḥ: Strength or energy (also wealth).
prasavaḥ: Progeny, offspring, or productivity.
me: Refers to for me (as in, let these qualities be mine).

contentBlocks.translation: May I have determination and accomplishment.
contentBlocks.meanings:
prayatiśca me prasitiśca me: May I have determination and accomplishment.
prayatiḥ: Determination or perseverance.
prasitiḥ: Success or accomplishment.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have both wisdom and willpower.
contentBlocks.meanings:
dhītiśca me kratuśca me: May I have both wisdom and willpower.
dhītiḥ: Wisdom or intelligence.
kratuḥ: Willpower or resolve.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have musical expression and fame.
contentBlocks.meanings:
svaraśca me ślokaśca me: May I have musical expression and fame.
svaraḥ: Sound or tone, often referring to vocal expression or music.
ślokaḥ: Fame, renown, or verses (hymns).

contentBlocks.translation: May I have good reputation and understanding of sacred knowledge.
contentBlocks.meanings:
śrāvaśca me śrutiśca me: May I have good reputation and understanding of sacred knowledge.
śrāvaḥ: Reputation, fame, or hearing.
śrutiḥ: Sacred knowledge or scripture (that which is heard, particularly the Vedas).

contentBlocks.translation: May I have light and the celestial realm of heaven.
contentBlocks.meanings:
jyotiśca me suvaśca me: May I have light and heaven.
jyotiḥ: Light or illumination.
suvaḥ: Heaven or celestial realms.

contentBlocks.translation: May my vital life forces be strong, both the outgoing and incoming breath.
contentBlocks.meanings:
prāṇaśca me'pānaśca me: May my vital life forces (breath) be strong, both outgoing and incoming.
prāṇaḥ: Outgoing breath or life force.
apānaḥ: Incoming breath or life force.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have balanced energy flow and perfect vitality.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vyānaśca me'suśca me: May I have balanced energy flow and perfect health.
vyānaḥ: A type of vital breath that governs circulation of energy within the body.
asuḥ: Life or vitality.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have a focused mind and retained knowledge.
contentBlocks.meanings:
cittaṃ ca ma ādhītaṃ ca me: May I have a focused mind and retained knowledge.
cittam: Mind or consciousness.
ādhītam: Retained or acquired knowledge.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the power of speech and control of the mind.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vākca me manaśca me: May I have the power of speech and control of the mind.
vāk: Speech or voice.
manaḥ: Mind or thought.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have sharp vision and keen hearing.
contentBlocks.meanings:
cakṣuśca me śrotraṃ ca me: May I have sharp vision and hearing.
cakṣuḥ: Eyes or vision.
śrotram: Ears or hearing.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have skill and strength.
contentBlocks.meanings:
dakṣaḥ: Skill or capability.
balaṃ: Strength.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have vitality and endurance.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ojaḥ: Vitality or vigor.
sahaḥ: Endurance or power.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have longevity and graceful aging.
contentBlocks.meanings:
āyuśca me jarā ca ma: May I have longevity and graceful aging.
āyuḥ: Longevity or life.
jarā: Old age.

contentBlocks.translation: May my soul and body be strong.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ātmā ca me tanūśca me: May my soul and body be strong.
ātmā: Soul or self.
tanūḥ: Body.

contentBlocks.original: शर्म॑ च मे॒ वर्म॑ च॒ मे-ऽङ्गा॑नि च मे॒-ऽस्थानि॑ च मे॒
contentBlocks.transliteration: śarma̍ cha mē̠ varma̍ cha̠ mē-'ṅgā̍ni cha mē̠-'sthāni̍ cha mē̠
contentBlocks.translation: May I have protection, a shield, strong limbs, and sturdy bones.
contentBlocks.meanings:
śarma ca me varma ca me: May I have protection and a shield.
śarma: Protection or happiness.
varma: Shield or armor.
aṅgāni ca me'sthāni ca me: May my limbs and bones be strong.
aṅgāni: Limbs or body parts.
asthāni: Bones.

contentBlocks.original: परूग्ं॑षि च मे॒ शरी॑राणि च मे ॥ १॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: parūg̍ṃṣi cha mē̠ śarī̍rāṇi cha mē ..1..
contentBlocks.translation: May my joints and entire body be in good health.
contentBlocks.meanings:
parūgͫṣi ca me śarīrāṇi ca me: May my joints and body be in good health.
parūgͫṣi: Joints or connections.
śarīrāṇi: Body.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have supremacy and leadership.
contentBlocks.meanings:
jyaiṣṭhyaṃ: Supremacy, superiority, or being the best.
ādhipatyaṃ: Leadership or lordship.

contentBlocks.original: म॒न्युश्च॑ मे॒ भाम॑श्च॒ मे-ऽम॑श्च॒ मे-ऽम्भ॑श्च मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: ma̠nyuścha̍ mē̠ bhāma̍ścha̠ mē-'ma̍ścha̠ mē-'mbha̍ścha mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I have righteous anger, brilliance, wealth, and abundance of water.
contentBlocks.meanings:
manyuḥ: Righteous anger, or the ability to take determined action.
bhāmaḥ: Brilliance or radiance (both physically and mentally).
amaḥ: Wealth or resources.
ambhaḥ: Water or abundance, often referring to life's sustaining element.

contentBlocks.original: जे॒मा च॑ मे महि॒मा च॑ मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: jē̠mā cha̍ mē mahi̠mā cha̍ mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I have prosperity and greatness.
contentBlocks.meanings:
jemā: Prosperity, well-being, or excellence.
mahimā: Greatness or magnificence.

contentBlocks.original: वरि॒मा च॑ मे प्रथि॒मा च॑ मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: vari̠mā cha̍ mē prathi̠mā cha̍ mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I have vastness and expansiveness.
contentBlocks.meanings:
varimā: Vastness or greatness in extent.
prathimā: Expansiveness or extension in all directions.

contentBlocks.original: व॒र्​ष्मा च॑ मे द्राघु॒या च॑ मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: va̠r​ṣmā cha̍ mē drāghu̠yā cha̍ mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I have height and length.
contentBlocks.meanings:
varṣmā: Height or stature.
drāghuyā: Length or long life.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have growth and increase.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vṛddham: Growth or prosperity (refers to material or spiritual growth).
vṛddhiḥ: Increase or improvement.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have truth and faith.
contentBlocks.meanings:
satyaḥ: Truth or righteousness.
śraddhā: Faith or devotion.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have dominion over the world and wealth.
contentBlocks.meanings:
jagat: The world or universe.
dhanam: Wealth or riches.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have control and splendor.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vaśaḥ: Control, authority, or influence.
tviṣiḥ: Radiance or brilliance.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have joy and delight.
contentBlocks.meanings:
krīḍā: Playfulness or joy.
modaḥ: Delight or happiness.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have what is already born and what is yet to be born.
contentBlocks.meanings:
jātam: That which is already born or created.
janiṣyamāṇaṃ: That which is yet to be born or created.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have well-spoken words and virtuous deeds.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sūktam: Well-spoken words or hymns.
sukṛtam: Virtuous deeds or good actions.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have wealth and knowledge.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vittam: Wealth or possessions.
vedyam: Knowledge or understanding (often refers to Vedic knowledge).

contentBlocks.translation: May I have what exists now and what is yet to come.
contentBlocks.meanings:
bhūtam: That which exists, or the present.
bhaviṣyam: That which is yet to come, or the future.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have ease of passage and a good path.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sugam: Ease or comfort (may also refer to an easy journey or passage).
supatham: A good or righteous path.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have prosperity and accomplishment.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ṛddham: Prosperity, wealth, or success.
ṛddhiḥ: Accomplishment or growth.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have completion and the ability to plan.
contentBlocks.meanings:
kḷptam: Completion or arrangement.
kḷptiḥ: Ability to plan or preparation.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have wisdom and good judgment.
contentBlocks.meanings:
matiḥ: Wisdom, intellect, or understanding.
sumatiḥ: Good judgment, right understanding, or favorable disposition.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have peace and prosperity.
contentBlocks.meanings:
śam: Peace or well-being.
mayaḥ: Prosperity or joy.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have that which is dear, that which fulfills my desires, and desires fulfilled.
contentBlocks.meanings:
priyam: That which is dear or loved.
anukāmaḥ: Fulfillment of desires or wishes.
kāmaḥ: Desires or wishes.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have peace of mind and auspiciousness.
contentBlocks.meanings:
saumanasaḥ: Peace of mind or good thoughts.
bhadram: Auspiciousness or good fortune.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have excellence and control.
contentBlocks.meanings:
śreyaḥ: Excellence, welfare, or prosperity.
vasyaḥ: Control or influence.

contentBlocks.original: यश॑श्च मे॒ भग॑श्च मे॒ द्रवि॑ण-ञ्च मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: yaśa̍ścha mē̠ bhaga̍ścha mē̠ dravi̍ṇa-ñcha mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I have fame, good fortune, and wealth.
contentBlocks.meanings:
yaśaḥ: Fame or renown.
bhagaḥ: Good fortune, prosperity.
draviṇam: Wealth or resources.

contentBlocks.original: य॒न्ता च॑ मे ध॒र्ता च॑ मे॒
contentBlocks.transliteration: ya̠ntā cha̍ mē dha̠rtā cha̍ mē̠
contentBlocks.translation: May I have guidance and support.
contentBlocks.meanings:
yantā: Guide or director.
dhartā: Supporter or sustainer.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have security and perseverance.
contentBlocks.meanings:
kṣemaḥ: Security or safety.
dhṛtiḥ: Perseverance, steadiness, or determination.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the whole universe and greatness.
contentBlocks.meanings:
viśvam: The whole universe or entirety.
mahaḥ: Greatness or magnificence.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have understanding and knowledge.
contentBlocks.meanings:
saṃvit: Understanding, awareness, or consciousness.
jñātram: Knowledge or insight.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have birth and productivity.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sūḥ: Birth or creation.
prasūḥ: Productivity or fruitfulness.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have firmness and dissolution.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sīram: Firmness or stability.
layaḥ: Dissolution or rest.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have truth, immortality, freedom from disease, and health.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ṛtam: Truth or cosmic order.
amṛtam: Immortality or the nectar of immortality.
yakṣmam: Disease or affliction.
anāmayam: Health, freedom from disease.

contentBlocks.original: जी॒वातु॑श्च मे दीर्घायु॒त्व-ञ्च॑ मे-ऽनमि॒त्र-ञ्च॒ मे-ऽभ॑य-ञ्च मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: jī̠vātu̍ścha mē dīrghāyu̠tva-ñcha̍ mē-'nami̠tra-ñcha̠ mē-'bha̍ya-ñcha mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I have vitality, longevity, freedom from enemies, and fearlessness.
contentBlocks.meanings:
jīvātuḥ: Vitality or life.
dīrghāyutvam: Long life or longevity.
anamitram: Without enemies.
abhayam: Fearlessness or safety.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have comfort and restful sleep.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sugam: Comfort or ease.
śayanam: Sleep or a bed to rest.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have good mornings and auspicious days.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sūṣā: Morning or dawn.
sudinam: An auspicious or good day.

contentBlocks.original:
4th Anuvaka
ऊर्क्च॑ मे सू॒नृता॑ च मे॒
contentBlocks.transliteration:
4th Anuvaka
ūrkcha̍ mē sū̠nṛtā̍ cha mē̠
contentBlocks.translation: May I have nourishment and sweet speech.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ūrk: Nourishment or strength.
sūnṛtā: Sweet speech, truthfulness, or pleasing words.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have milk and juice (essence).
contentBlocks.meanings:
payaḥ: Milk, often symbolizing nourishment.
rasaḥ: Juice or essence, referring to vitality and taste.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have clarified butter and honey.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ghṛtam: Clarified butter (ghee), a symbol of purity and nourishment.
madhu: Honey, representing sweetness and abundance.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have thick cream and nourishment.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sagdhiḥ: Thick cream or curd, another nourishing substance.
sapītiḥ: Nourishment or sustenance.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have agriculture and rain.
contentBlocks.meanings:
kṛṣiḥ: Agriculture or farming.
vṛṣṭiḥ: Rain, essential for crops and fertility.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have victory and sprouting (growth).
contentBlocks.meanings:
jaitram: Victory or success.
audbhidyam: Growth or sprouting, particularly of plants.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have wealth and abundance.
contentBlocks.meanings:
rayiḥ: Wealth or possessions.
rāyaḥ: Abundance or prosperity.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have fullness and nourishment.
contentBlocks.meanings:
puṣṭam: Fullness or completeness.
puṣṭiḥ: Nourishment or thriving.

contentBlocks.original: वि॒भु च॑ मे प्र॒भु च॑ मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: vi̠bhu cha̍ mē pra̠bhu cha̍ mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I be granted the gift of abundance of all varieties of cereals, millets and corn.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vibhu ca me: May I have abundance.
prabhu ca me: May I have great plenty.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have plenty and increase.
contentBlocks.meanings:
bahu: Plenty or abundance.
bhūyaḥ: Increase or growth.

contentBlocks.original: पू॒र्ण-ञ्च॑ मे पू॒र्णत॑र-ञ्च॒ मे-ऽक्षि॑तिश्च मे॒ कूय॑वाश्च॒ मे-ऽन्न॑-ञ्च॒ मे-ऽक्षु॑च्च मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: pū̠rṇa-ñcha̍ mē pū̠rṇata̍ra-ñcha̠ mē-'kṣi̍tiścha mē̠ kūya̍vāścha̠ mē-'nna̍-ñcha̠ mē-'kṣu̍chcha mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I have fullness and greater fullness, land and heaps of grain, food and sugarcane.
contentBlocks.meanings:
pūrṇam: Fullness or completeness.
pūrṇataram: Greater fullness or even more completeness.
kṣitiḥ: Land or earth.
kūyavāḥ: Heaps of grain, symbolizing agricultural abundance.
annam: Food or nourishment.
akṣu: Sugarcane, a symbol of sweetness and prosperity.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have rice and barley.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vrīhayaḥ: Rice, a staple grain in many cultures.
yavāḥ: Barley, another important grain.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have black gram and sesame.
contentBlocks.meanings:
māṣāḥ: Black gram, a type of legume.
tilāḥ: Sesame seeds, often used for oil and food.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have green gram and millet.
contentBlocks.meanings:
mudgāḥ: Green gram (mung beans).
khalvāḥ: Millet, another grain.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have wheat and lentils.
contentBlocks.meanings:
godhūmāḥ: Wheat, an essential grain.
masurāḥ: Lentils, a staple pulse.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have long pepper and small grains.
contentBlocks.meanings:
priyaṅgavaḥ: Long pepper, a medicinal and culinary spice.
aṇavaḥ: Small grains or minute particles.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have wild rice and other grains.
contentBlocks.meanings:
śyāmakāḥ: A type of grain, possibly wild rice or millet.
nīvārāḥ: Wild rice or another type of coarse grain.

contentBlocks.original:
5th Anuvaka
अश्मा॑ च मे॒ मृत्ति॑का च मे
contentBlocks.transliteration:
5th Anuvaka
aśmā̍ cha mē̠ mṛtti̍kā cha mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I have stone and clay.
contentBlocks.meanings:
aśmā: Stone or rock.
mṛttikā: Clay or earth, often used for making pottery or bricks.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have mountains and hills.
contentBlocks.meanings:
girayaḥ: Mountains.
parvatāḥ: Hills, similar to mountains but usually smaller in scale.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have sand and forest trees.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sikatāḥ: Sand or gravel.
vanaspatayaḥ: Forest trees or large trees that grow in the wild.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have gold and iron.
contentBlocks.meanings:
hiraṇyam: Gold, a symbol of wealth and purity.
ayaḥ: Iron, representing strength and utility.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have lead and tin.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sīsam: Lead, a heavy metal.
trapuḥ: Tin, often used in alloys like bronze.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have black iron, copper, and fire.
contentBlocks.meanings:
śyāmam: Black iron, possibly referring to steel or a type of iron.
loham: Copper, a valuable metal used for various purposes.
agniḥ: Fire, a sacred element in Vedic rituals.

contentBlocks.original: आप॑श्च मे वी॒रुध॑श्च म॒ ओष॑धयश्च मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: āpa̍ścha mē vī̠rudha̍ścha ma̠ ōṣa̍dhayaścha mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I have water, herbs, and medicinal plants.
contentBlocks.meanings:
āpaḥ: Water, essential for life and purification.
vīrudhaḥ: Herbs or small plants.
oṣadhayaḥ: Medicinal plants or herbs used for healing.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have cultivated and uncultivated food.
contentBlocks.meanings:
kṛṣṭapacyaṃ: Cultivated crops or food that is grown.
akṛṣṭapacyaṃ: Wild or uncultivated food, food that grows naturally without farming.

contentBlocks.original: ग्रा॒म्याश्च॑ मे प॒शव॑ आर॒ण्याश्च॑ य॒ज्ञेन॑ कल्पन्तां
contentBlocks.transliteration: grā̠myāścha̍ mē pa̠śava̍ āra̠ṇyāścha̍ ya̠jñēna̍ kalpantāṃ
contentBlocks.translation: May I have domesticated and wild animals, and may they be fit for sacrifice.
contentBlocks.meanings:
grāmyāḥ: Domesticated animals, often referring to cattle or livestock.
āraṇyāḥ: Wild animals, those that live in the forest or wilderness.
yajñena: Through sacrifice or ritual.
kalpantām: Fit or appropriate, usually in the context of being used in rituals.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have wealth and the means of acquiring wealth.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vittam: Wealth or riches.
vittiḥ: The means or resources for acquiring wealth.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have what exists and prosperity.
contentBlocks.meanings:
bhūtam: That which exists, or present possessions.
bhūtiḥ: Prosperity or success.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have wealth and a dwelling place.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vasu: Wealth or riches.
vasatiḥ: Dwelling place, home, or residence.

contentBlocks.original: कर्म॑ च मे॒ शक्ति॑श्च॒ मे-ऽर्थ॑श्च म॒
contentBlocks.transliteration: karma̍ cha mē̠ śakti̍ścha̠ mē-'rtha̍ścha ma̠
contentBlocks.translation: May I have action, strength, and purpose.
contentBlocks.meanings:
karma: Action, deed, or work.
śaktiḥ: Strength or power.
arthaḥ: Purpose, or wealth.

contentBlocks.original: एम॑श्च म इति॑श्च मे॒ गति॑श्च मे ॥ ५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: ēma̍ścha ma iti̍ścha mē̠ gati̍ścha mē ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May I have acquisition, completeness, and progress.
contentBlocks.meanings:
emaḥ: Acquisition or possession.
itiḥ: Completion or conclusion.
gatiḥ: Movement, progress, or the ability to advance.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of Agni and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
agniśca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of Agni and Indra.
agniḥ: The fire god, representing purity and transformation.
indraḥ: The king of the gods, symbolizing strength and leadership.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of Soma and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
somaśca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of Soma and Indra.
somaḥ: The moon and the divine nectar, symbolizing immortality and inspiration.
indraḥ: Indra, again called upon for his might and authority.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of Savitā and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
savitā ca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of Savitā and Indra.
savitā: The sun god, who stimulates all life and represents illumination and energy.
indraḥ: Indra, invoked again for protection and strength.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of Sarasvatī and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sarasvatī ca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of Sarasvatī and Indra.
sarasvatī: The goddess of wisdom, learning, and the arts.
indraḥ: Indra, continuing to provide power and dominance.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of Pūṣā and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
pūṣā ca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of Pūṣā and Indra.
pūṣā: The god of prosperity, nourishment, and well-being.
indraḥ: Indra, for his protective qualities.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of Bṛhaspati and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
bṛhaspatiśca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of Bṛhaspati and Indra.
bṛhaspatiḥ: The teacher of the gods, representing wisdom and guidance.
indraḥ: Indra, called again to support with his strength and leadership.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of Mitra and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
mitraśca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of Mitra and Indra.
mitraḥ: The god of friendship and harmony, often associated with contracts and agreements.
indraḥ: Indra, once more for his might and sovereignty.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of Varuṇa and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
varuṇaśca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of Varuṇa and Indra.
varuṇaḥ: The god of the waters and cosmic law, symbolizing moral and physical order.
indraḥ: Indra, continuing to provide power and defense.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of Tvaṣṭā and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
tvaṣṭā ca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of Tvaṣṭā and Indra.
tvaṣṭā: The divine artisan or craftsman, representing creation and construction.
indraḥ: Indra, as usual, for his strength and leadership.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of Dhātā and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
dhātā ca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of Dhātā and Indra.
dhātā: The sustainer, one who upholds the universe and life.
indraḥ: Indra, for continued protection and leadership.

contentBlocks.original: विष्णु॑श्च म॒ इन्द्र॑श्च मे-ऽश्विनौ॑ च म॒ इन्द्र॑श्च मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: viṣṇu̍ścha ma̠ indra̍ścha mē-'śvinau̍ cha ma̠ indra̍ścha mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of Viṣṇu and Indra, and of the Aśvin twins and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
viṣṇuśca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of Viṣṇu and Indra.
viṣṇuḥ: The preserver of the universe, representing balance and continuity.
indraḥ: Indra, invoked here again for his authority and might.
aśvinau ca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of the Aśvin twins and Indra.
aśvinau: The divine twin horsemen, representing healing and protection.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of the Maruts and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
marutaśca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of the Maruts and Indra.
marutaḥ: The storm gods, who bring energy, change, and transformation.
indraḥ: Indra, for his strength to lead the divine forces.

contentBlocks.original: विश्वे॑ च मे दे॒वा इन्द्र॑श्च मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: viśvē̍ cha mē dē̠vā indra̍ścha mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of all the gods and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
viśve ca me devā indraśca me: May I have the blessings of all the gods and Indra.
viśve devāḥ: All the gods, a collective invocation for the blessings of the entire pantheon.
indraḥ: Indra, the leader among the gods.

contentBlocks.original: पृथि॒वी च॑ म॒ इन्द्र॑श्च मे-ऽन्तरि॑क्ष-ञ्च म॒ इन्द्र॑श्च मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: pṛthi̠vī cha̍ ma̠ indra̍ścha mē-'ntari̍kṣa-ñcha ma̠ indra̍ścha mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of the Earth and Indra, and of the atmosphere and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
pṛthivī ca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of the Earth and Indra.
pṛthivī: The Earth goddess, symbolizing fertility, stability, and support.
indraḥ: Indra, who protects and governs.
'ntarikṣaṃ ca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of the atmosphere and Indra.
antarikṣam: The intermediate space, the atmosphere or sky.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of the sky and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
dyauśca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of the sky and Indra.
dyauḥ: The sky, representing the higher realms or the celestial sphere.
indraḥ: Indra, the king of the gods who resides in the heavens.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of the directions and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
diśaśca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of the directions and Indra.
diśaḥ: The directions or quarters, which govern the various parts of the world.
indraḥ: Indra, who governs all directions as the king of the gods.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of the cosmic summit (head) and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
mūrdhā ca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of the head (cosmic summit) and Indra.
mūrdhā: The head or summit, possibly referring to the highest point in creation, both physical and cosmic.
indraḥ: Indra, overseeing the highest realms.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of Prajāpati and Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings:
prajāpatiśca ma indraśca me: May I have the blessings of Prajāpati and Indra.
prajāpatiḥ: The lord of all creatures, representing the source of creation.
indraḥ: Indra, who governs and protects the world.

contentBlocks.original:
7th Anuvaka
अ॒ग्ं॒शुश्च॑ मे र॒श्मिश्च॒ मे-ऽदा᳚भ्यश्च॒ मे-ऽधि॑पतिश्च म
contentBlocks.transliteration:
7th Anuvaka
a̠gṃ̠śuścha̍ mē ra̠śmiścha̠ mē-'dā̎bhyaścha̠ mē-'dhi̍patiścha ma
contentBlocks.translation: May I have the Agṃśu vessel, the Raśmi vessel, the Adābhya vessel, and the Adhipati vessel.
contentBlocks.meanings:
agṃśuḥ: A small Soma vessel used in Vedic rituals.
raśmiḥ: Another vessel associated with Soma or the rays (reins) of light.
dābhyaḥ: A vessel used in specific sacrificial offerings.
adhipatiḥ: A vessel for the chief offering, symbolizing control or lordship in the ritual.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the Upāgṃśu vessel and the Antaryāma vessel.
contentBlocks.meanings:
upāgṃśuḥ: A small vessel used in the Soma sacrifice, typically to hold or pour Soma juice.
antaryāmaḥ: Another vessel related to Soma, used in intermediate stages of the sacrifice.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the Aindravāyava vessel and the Maitrāvaruṇa vessel.
contentBlocks.meanings:
aindravāyavaḥ: A vessel used in sacrifices to Indra and Vāyu (wind god).
maitrāvaruṇaḥ: A vessel used in rituals related to Mitra and Varuṇa, gods of contracts and cosmic order.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the Āśvina vessel and the Pratiprasthāna vessel.
contentBlocks.meanings:
āśvinaḥ: A vessel associated with the Aśvins, the twin gods of healing.
pratiprasthānaḥ: A vessel or item used at the concluding part of the ritual.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the Śukra vessel and the Manthī vessel.
contentBlocks.meanings:
śukraḥ: A vessel associated with purity, often used in rituals related to Soma.
manthī: The vessel used for churning the Soma during the sacrifice.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the Āgrayaṇa vessel and the Vaiśvadeva vessel.
contentBlocks.meanings:
āgrayaṇaḥ: A vessel for the first offering of the year or the first-fruits sacrifice.
vaiśvadevaḥ: A vessel used in offerings to all the gods (Viśve Devāḥ).

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the Dhruva vessel and the Vaiśvānara vessel.
contentBlocks.meanings:
dhruvaḥ: A fixed or stable vessel used in Vedic rituals, symbolizing permanence.
vaiśvānaraḥ: A vessel used in sacrifices to Agni (the universal fire).

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the seasonal libation vessels and the additional libation vessels.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ṛtugrahāḥ: Vessels used for offerings made during specific seasons or seasonal rites.
atigrāhyāḥ: Vessels used for special or additional libations.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the Aindrāgna vessel and the Vaiśvadeva vessel.
contentBlocks.meanings:
aindrāgnaḥ: A vessel used in sacrifices dedicated to both Indra and Agni.
vaiśvadevaḥ: A vessel used for the universal offering to all the gods (Viśve Devāḥ).

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the Marutvatīya vessel and the Māhendra vessel.
contentBlocks.meanings:
marutvatīyaḥ: A vessel used in sacrifices related to the Maruts, the storm gods.
māhendraḥ: A vessel used in sacrifices dedicated to the great Indra.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the Āditya vessel and the Sāvitra vessel.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ādityaḥ: A vessel used in sacrifices to the Ādityas (solar deities).
sāvitraḥ: A vessel associated with the sun god Sāvitrī.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the Sārasvata vessel and the Pauṣṇa vessel.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sārasvataḥ: A vessel associated with Sarasvatī, the goddess of wisdom and learning.
pauṣṇaḥ: A vessel associated with Pūṣan, the god of prosperity and nourishment.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the Pātnīvata vessel and the Hāriyojana vessel.
contentBlocks.meanings:
pātnīvataḥ: A vessel used in rituals that involve the gods along with their consorts (gods who have wives).
hāriyojanaḥ: A vessel associated with deities that move in golden chariots, such as Pūṣan or the Aśvins.

contentBlocks.translation: May I be blessed with fuel wood and sacred grass for the ritual.
contentBlocks.meanings:
idhmaḥ: Fuel wood, used for feeding the sacrificial fire.
barhiḥ: Sacred grass, specifically the kuśa grass, used in Vedic rituals.

contentBlocks.translation: May I be blessed with the altar and the sacrificial hearths for the ritual.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vediḥ: The altar, where Vedic rituals and sacrifices are performed.
dhiṣṇiyāḥ: The sacrificial hearth or place where the sacred fire is kept.

contentBlocks.translation: May I be blessed with the sacrificial ladles and the cups used in the ritual.
contentBlocks.meanings:
srucaḥ: Sacrificial ladles, used for offering ghee or other offerings into the fire.
camasāḥ: Cups or vessels used to hold Soma or other offerings during rituals.

contentBlocks.translation: May I be blessed with the pressing stones and the ritual invocations.
contentBlocks.meanings:
grāvāṇaḥ: Pressing stones, used to extract Soma juice during sacrifices.
svaravaḥ: Invocations or hymns, particularly those sung or chanted during rituals.

contentBlocks.translation: May I be blessed with the lower altar and the pressing boards used in the ritual.
contentBlocks.meanings:
uparavāḥ: A lower or secondary altar used during sacrifices.
adhiṣavaṇe: Refers to the pressing stones used in preparing Soma during rituals.

contentBlocks.translation: May I be blessed with the large ritual vessels and the strainers used in the ceremony.
contentBlocks.meanings:
droṇakalaśaḥ: Large vessels or pots used to hold Soma or other ritual substances.
vāyavyāni: Strainers, used for filtering Soma or other liquids during rituals.

contentBlocks.translation: May I be blessed with the carrier of purified offerings and the sacrificial spoon.
contentBlocks.meanings:
pūtabhṛt: A vessel or carrier that holds purified offerings.
ādhavanīyaḥ: A spoon used in rituals for pouring offerings into the sacred fire.

contentBlocks.translation: May I be blessed with the fire altar and the altar where offerings are placed.
contentBlocks.meanings:
āgnīdhraṃ: The fire altar or the priest responsible for maintaining the sacred fire.
havirdhānam: The altar or place where offerings (havis) are made during the ritual.

contentBlocks.original: गृ॒हाश्च॑ मे॒ सद॑श्च मे पुरो॒डाशा᳚श्च मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: gṛ̠hāścha̍ mē̠ sada̍ścha mē purō̠ḍāśā̎ścha mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I be blessed with the ritual house, the sacred seat, and the sacrificial cakes.
contentBlocks.meanings:
gṛhāḥ: The ritual house or space where sacrifices and rituals are performed.
sadaḥ: The sacred seat, often referring to the seat of the priests or the space reserved for rituals.
puroḍāśaḥ: Sacrificial cakes offered during rituals, often made of rice or barley.

contentBlocks.translation: May I be blessed with the cooked offerings, the final purification ceremony, and the invocation of well-being.
contentBlocks.meanings:
pacatāḥ: Cooked offerings, prepared as part of the sacrifice.
avabhṛthaḥ: The final purification or bathing ceremony performed at the end of a ritual.
svagākāraḥ: The invocation or chant for well-being and auspiciousness, often performed at the conclusion of a ritual.

contentBlocks.original:
9th Anuvaka
अ॒ग्निश्च॑ मे घ॒र्मश्च॑ मे॒-ऽर्कश्च॑ मे॒
contentBlocks.transliteration:
9th Anuvaka
a̠gniścha̍ mē gha̠rmaścha̍ mē̠-'rkaścha̍ mē̠
contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of Agni (fire), warmth, and radiant light.
contentBlocks.meanings:
agniḥ: The fire god, representing purification and energy.
gharmaḥ: Warmth, often referring to heat from the sacrificial fire.
ṛkaḥ: Light or radiance, possibly referring to the light of fire or the sun.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of the Sun, the life force (breath), and the Ashvamedha sacrifice.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sūryaḥ: The Sun god, symbolizing life, vitality, and illumination.
prāṇaḥ: The vital breath or life force, essential for life.
aśvamedhaḥ: The Ashvamedha, a powerful Vedic horse sacrifice symbolizing sovereignty and abundance.

contentBlocks.original: पृथि॒वी च॒ मे-ऽदि॑तिश्च मे॒ दिति॑श्च मे॒
contentBlocks.transliteration: pṛthi̠vī cha̠ mē-'di̍tiścha mē̠ diti̍ścha mē̠
contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of the Earth, Aditi, and Diti.
contentBlocks.meanings:
pṛthivī: The Earth, representing stability, fertility, and sustenance.
aditiḥ: The mother of the gods, symbolizing infinite space, freedom, and support.
ditiḥ: Diti, associated with material desires and the mother of demons.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of the Sky, the śakvarī meter, the fingers, and the directions.
contentBlocks.meanings:
dyauḥ: The Sky, representing the celestial or heavenly realms.
śakkvarīḥ: A meter used in Vedic hymns, symbolizing rhythm.
aṅgulayaḥ: Fingers, often referring to precise actions during rituals.
diśaḥ: The directions, representing orientation and guidance.

contentBlocks.translation: May the Rig Veda and all these be fulfilled for me through sacrifice.
contentBlocks.meanings: ṛk: Refers to the Rig Veda, the oldest of the Vedic scriptures.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of the Sama Veda and hymns of praise.
contentBlocks.meanings:
sāma: Refers to the Sama Veda, which is associated with melodies and music in the Vedic rituals.
stomaḥ: Hymns of praise or eulogies.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of the Yajur Veda and consecration.
contentBlocks.meanings:
yajuḥ: Refers to the Yajur Veda, focusing on the prose mantras used in rituals.
dīkṣā: Consecration or initiation, a purification ritual.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of austerity and the seasons.
contentBlocks.meanings:
tapaḥ: Austerity, penance, or spiritual discipline.
ṛtuḥ: Seasons, symbolizing the natural cycle and order.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of vows, day and night, rain, and the great Bṛhat and Rathantara cycles.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vratam: Vows or observances, symbolizing discipline and dedication.
ahorātrayoḥ: Day and night, representing the passage of time.
vṛṣṭyā: Rain, symbolizing fertility and life-sustaining water.
bṛhadrathantare: A great cycle or intermediary period, often referring to significant cosmic or ritual cycles.

contentBlocks.translation: May all these be fulfilled for me through sacrifice.
contentBlocks.meanings: yajñena kalpetām: May these be accomplished or fulfilled through the power of sacrifice.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of pregnancies and calves.
contentBlocks.meanings:
garbhāḥ: Pregnancies or unborn offspring, symbolizing fertility.
vatsāḥ: Calves, representing young animals and abundance in livestock.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of triplet-bearing males and triplet-bearing females.
contentBlocks.meanings:
tryaviḥ: A male that produces or accompanies triplets (usually cattle).
tryavī: A female that gives birth to triplets, symbolizing abundance.

contentBlocks.original: दित्य॒वाट् च॑ मे दित्यौ॒ही च॑ मे॒
contentBlocks.transliteration: ditya̠vāṭ cha̍ mē dityau̠hī cha̍ mē̠
contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of quadruplet-bearing males and quadruplet-bearing females.
contentBlocks.meanings:
dityavāṭ: A male that produces or is associated with quadruplets.
dityauhī: A female that gives birth to quadruplets.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of quintuplet-bearing males and quintuplet-bearing females.
contentBlocks.meanings:
pañcāviḥ: A male producing or accompanying quintuplets.
pañcāvī: A female that bears quintuplets.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of three-year-old male and female cattle.
contentBlocks.meanings:
trivatsaḥ: A three-year-old male, especially a bull or ox.
trivatsā: A three-year-old female, symbolizing strength and maturity in livestock.

contentBlocks.original: तुर्य॒वाट् च॑ मे तुर्यौ॒ही च॑ मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: turya̠vāṭ cha̍ mē turyau̠hī cha̍ mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of four-year-old male and female cattle.
contentBlocks.meanings:
turyavāṭ: A four-year-old male.
turyauhī: A four-year-old female.

contentBlocks.original: पष्ठ॒वाट् च॑ मे पष्ठौ॒ही च॑ म
contentBlocks.transliteration: paṣṭha̠vāṭ cha̍ mē paṣṭhau̠hī cha̍ ma
contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of six-year-old male and female cattle.
contentBlocks.meanings:
paṣṭhavāṭ: A six-year-old male.
paṣṭhauhī: A six-year-old female.

contentBlocks.original: उ॒क्षा च॑ मे व॒शा च॑ म
contentBlocks.transliteration: u̠kṣā cha̍ mē va̠śā cha̍ ma
contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of a bull and a cow.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ukṣā: A bull, symbolizing strength and virility.
vaśā: A cow, symbolizing nurturing and abundance.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of a bull, a strong male, and a serviceable bull.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ṛṣabhaḥ: A bull, representing leadership and fertility.
vehacca: A powerful or strong male.
naḍvāñ: A bull, possibly referring to a domesticated or serviceable one.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have the blessings of a milking cow, and may my life be fulfilled through sacrifice.
contentBlocks.meanings:
dhenuḥ: A milking cow, representing sustenance and wealth.
āyuḥ: Life or longevity.
yajñena kalpatām: May it be fulfilled through sacrifice or ritual offerings.

contentBlocks.translation: May my vital in-breath and out-breath be fulfilled through sacrifice.
contentBlocks.meanings:
prāṇaḥ: Breath or vital force.
apānaḥ: Exhalation or outward breath.
yajñena kalpatām: Fulfilled through sacrifice or rituals.

contentBlocks.translation: May my circulatory vital energy be fulfilled through sacrifice.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vyānaḥ: The vital energy that governs the circulation of energy in the body.
yajñena kalpatām: Fulfilled through sacrifice or rituals.

contentBlocks.translation: May my sight and hearing be fulfilled through sacrifice.
contentBlocks.meanings:
cakṣuḥ: Eyes or vision.
śrotraṃ: Ears or hearing.
yajñena kalpatām: Fulfilled through sacrifice.

contentBlocks.translation: May my mind be fulfilled through sacrifice.
contentBlocks.meanings:
manaḥ: Mind or thought.
yajñena kalpatām: Fulfilled through sacrifice or rituals.

contentBlocks.translation: May my speech and my soul be fulfilled through sacrifice.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vāk: Speech.
ātmā: The soul or self.
yajñena kalpatām: Fulfilled through sacrifice.

contentBlocks.translation: May the sacrifice itself be fulfilled through sacrifice.
contentBlocks.meanings:
yajñaḥ: Sacrifice or ritual offering.
yajñena kalpatām: Fulfilled through sacrifice or offerings.

contentBlocks.original:
11th Anuvaka
एका॑ च मे ति॒स्रश्च॑ मे॒
contentBlocks.transliteration:
11th Anuvaka
ēkā̍ cha mē ti̠sraścha̍ mē̠
contentBlocks.translation: May I have one and three.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ekā: One.
tisraḥ: Three.

contentBlocks.original: पञ्च॑ च मे स॒प्त च॑ मे॒
contentBlocks.transliteration: pañcha̍ cha mē sa̠pta cha̍ mē̠
contentBlocks.translation: May I have five and seven.
contentBlocks.meanings:
pañca: Five.
sapta: Seven.

contentBlocks.original: नव॑ च म॒ एका॑दश च मे॒
contentBlocks.transliteration: nava̍ cha ma̠ ēkā̍daśa cha mē̠
contentBlocks.translation: May I have nine and eleven.
contentBlocks.meanings:
nava: Nine.
ekādaśa: Eleven.

contentBlocks.original: त्रयो॑दश च मे॒ पञ्च॑दश च मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: trayō̍daśa cha mē̠ pañcha̍daśa cha mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I have thirteen and fifteen.
contentBlocks.meanings:
trayodaśa: Thirteen.
pañcadaśa: Fifteen.

contentBlocks.original: स॒प्तद॑श च मे॒ नव॑दश च म॒
contentBlocks.transliteration: sa̠ptada̍śa cha mē̠ nava̍daśa cha ma̠
contentBlocks.translation: May I have seventeen and nineteen.
contentBlocks.meanings:
saptadaśa: Seventeen.
navadaśa: Nineteen.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have twenty-one and twenty-three.
contentBlocks.meanings:
ekavigͫśati: Twenty-one.
trayovigͫśati: Twenty-three.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have twenty-five and twenty-seven.
contentBlocks.meanings:
pañcavigͫśati: Twenty-five.
saptavigͫśati: Twenty-seven.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have twenty-nine and thirty-one.
contentBlocks.meanings:
navavigͫśati: Twenty-nine.
ekatrigͫśa: Thirty-one.

contentBlocks.original: त्रय॑स्त्रिग्ंशच्च मे॒ चत॑स्रश्च मे॒-ऽष्टौ च॑ मे॒
contentBlocks.transliteration: traya̍strigṃśachcha mē̠ chata̍sraścha mē̠-'ṣṭau cha̍ mē̠
contentBlocks.translation: May I have thirty-three, four, and eight.
contentBlocks.meanings:
trayastrigͫśa: Thirty-three.
catasraḥ: Four.
aṣṭau: Eight.

contentBlocks.original: द्वाद॑श च मे॒ षोड॑श च मे
contentBlocks.transliteration: dvāda̍śa cha mē̠ ṣōḍa̍śa cha mē
contentBlocks.translation: May I have twelve and sixteen.
contentBlocks.meanings:
dvādaśa: Twelve.
ṣoḍaśa: Sixteen.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have twenty, twenty-four, and twenty-eight.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vigͫśati: Twenty.
caturvigͫśati: Twenty-four.
aṣṭāvigͫśati: Twenty-eight.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have thirty-two and thirty-six.
contentBlocks.meanings:
dvātrigͫśa: Thirty-two.
ṣaṭtrigͫśa: Thirty-six.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have forty, forty-four, and forty-eight.
contentBlocks.meanings:
catvarigͫśa: Forty.
catuścatvārigͫśa: Forty-four.
aṣṭācatvārigͫśa: Forty-eight.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have strength, progeny, and the ability to act.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vājaḥ: Strength, energy, or vigor.
prasavaḥ: Progeny, offspring, or fertility.
āpijaḥ: The ability to act or perform.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have willpower, heaven, and the summit.
contentBlocks.meanings:
kratuḥ: Willpower or resolution.
suvaḥ: Heaven, the celestial realm.
mūrdhā: The head or summit, the highest point.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have pervasiveness, the final resting place, and the ultimate end.
contentBlocks.meanings:
vyaśniyaḥ: Pervasiveness, spread over everything.
antyāyanaḥ: The final resting place or end destination.
antyaḥ: The end or ultimate.

contentBlocks.translation: May I have prosperity, dominion over the world, and its lordship.
contentBlocks.meanings:
bhauvanaḥ: Prosperity or well-being in the world.
bhuvanaḥ: The world or realm.
adhipatiḥ: Lordship or sovereignty.

contentBlocks.translation: Om — Ida, the invoker of gods, Manu, the institutor of sacrifice, and Brihaspati, the utterer of hymns of praise,
contentBlocks.meanings:
oṃ: the sacred syllable Om
iḍā: Ida, the goddess of devotion / invoker of gods
devahūḥ: one who invokes the gods
manuḥ: Manu, the progenitor of mankind
yajñanīḥ: institutor or leader of sacrifice
bṛhaspatiḥ: Brihaspati, lord of prayer and speech
ukthāmadāni: exhilarating hymns of praise

contentBlocks.translation: May all the gods and the earth, our mother, sing forth with auspicious speech.
contentBlocks.meanings:
śaṃsiṣat: may (he/they) proclaim or sing
viśve devāḥ: all the gods
sūktavācaḥ: those of auspicious/good speech
pṛthivī: the earth
mātā: mother

contentBlocks.translation: May you not harm me; I shall think sweetness, I shall be born into sweetness.
contentBlocks.meanings:
mā hiṃsīḥ: do not harm / injure me
madhu: honey, sweetness, bliss
maniṣye: I shall think / meditate upon
janiṣye: I shall be born / come into being

contentBlocks.translation: I shall speak sweetness, I shall declare sweetness.
contentBlocks.meanings:
madhu: sweetness, honey, bliss
vakṣyāmi: I shall speak / carry
vadiṣyāmi: I shall declare / proclaim

contentBlocks.translation: May I speak sweet, worthy-of-hearing speech to the gods and to men.
contentBlocks.meanings:
madhumatīm: full of sweetness, honey-sweet
devebhyaḥ: to / for the gods
vācam: speech, word
udyāsam: may I utter / speak forth
śuśrūṣeṇyām: worthy of being heard, fit to be listened to

contentBlocks.translation: And likewise to mankind.
contentBlocks.meanings:
manuṣyebhyaḥ: to / for human beings, mankind
tam: that (same speech)

contentBlocks.translation: May the gods protect me for glory; may the forefathers rejoice and approve.
contentBlocks.meanings:
mā: me
devāḥ: the gods
avantu: may they protect / favour
śobhāyai: for glory / brilliance / auspiciousness
pitaraḥ: the forefathers, ancestors
anumadantu: may they rejoice in approval / be pleased

contentBlocks.translation: Om — peace, peace, peace.
contentBlocks.meanings:
oṃ: the sacred syllable Om
śāntiḥ: peace, tranquility (repeated three times to invoke peace at the physical, mental, and spiritual levels)
